name: La Mola Trail
name_original: Vuelta por Espot — circular en el Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici
description: Jog the easy 2.7-mile La Mola Trail in Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici, featuring a stone bridge and Pyrenees views
region_intro: Jogging around Espot offers access to diverse landscapes within the Catalan Pyrenees, bordering the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park and Alt Pirineu Natural Park. The region features a variety of terrains, from accessible valley paths to challenging high-altitude trails. Runners can explore mountainous environments, serene mountain lakes, and lush forests. Many routes provide opportunities to experience elevations exceeding 6,500 feet (2,000 meters). Best jogging routes around Espot The most popular jogging route is Estany de Sant Maurici y Estany Negre de Peguera — circular desde Espot en el Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici, a 17.3 miles (27.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 36 minutes to complete. This difficult route features challenging alpine terrain with significant elevation gain. Another top favourite among local runners is Tuc de Ratera Loop from Estany de Sant Maurici, a difficult 13.2 miles (21.3 km) path. This route offers expansive views and considerable elevation gain through the Pyrenees. Local runners also love the La Mola Trail, a 2.7 miles (4.3 km) trail leading through varied natural landscapes, often completed in about 28 minutes. Jogging around Espot is defined by high mountain peaks, serene lakes, and verdant forests. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy paths to demanding alpine trails. The routes in Espot are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 360 runners have used komoot to explore Espot's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Espot?
A: Espot offers a wide selection of running routes, with over 20 different options available for you to explore. These routes cater to various fitness levels, from easy paths to challenging alpine trails.

Q: Are there any easy running routes for beginners in Espot?
A: Yes, Espot has accessible paths suitable for beginners. For a relatively flat and shorter option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275029097/la-mola-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Mola Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 2.7 miles (4.3 km) long and can be completed in about 28 minutes.

Q: What are some challenging trail running options for experienced runners in Espot?
A: For experienced trail runners seeking a challenge, Espot offers numerous difficult routes with significant elevation gains. Two popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275106786/estany-de-sant-maurici-y-estany-negre-de-peguera-circular-desde-espot-en-el-parque-nacional-de-aigueestortes-i-sant-maurici" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estany de Sant Maurici y Estany Negre de Peguera — circular desde Espot&lt;/a&gt;, a 17.3-mile (27.8 km) route with challenging alpine terrain, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1274149263/tuc-de-ratera-loop-from-estany-de-sant-maurici" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tuc de Ratera Loop from Estany de Sant Maurici&lt;/a&gt;, a 13.2-mile (21.3 km) path offering expansive views and considerable elevation gain.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I expect to see while running in Espot?
A: Running in Espot provides access to stunning natural landscapes. You'll encounter picturesque mountain lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154020" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Sant Maurici&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/197506" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ratera Lake&lt;/a&gt;, as well as lush forests and high mountain peaks like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/325346" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico de Ratera&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes offer breathtaking viewpoints, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/154043" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Estany de Sant Maurici&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there many circular running routes in the Espot area?
A: Yes, the Espot region features several circular running routes, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1275106786/estany-de-sant-maurici-y-estany-negre-de-peguera-circular-desde-espot-en-el-parque-nacional-de-aigueestortes-i-sant-maurici" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Estany de Sant Maurici y Estany Negre de Peguera&lt;/a&gt; is a notable circular option, and the region's trail network includes various loops through its diverse landscapes.

Q: Is Espot a good destination for high-altitude running?
A: Absolutely. Espot is nestled in the Catalan Pyrenees, offering many paths that traverse stunning alpine environments, with routes often exceeding 6,500 feet (2,000 meters) in altitude. This provides a unique training experience with fresh air and expansive views, ideal for high-altitude running.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about the trails in Espot?
A: The running routes in Espot are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars. Runners frequently praise the stunning natural landscapes, the variety of terrain from accessible paths to demanding alpine trails, and the opportunity to run amidst high mountain peaks, serene lakes, and verdant forests.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by mountain refuges or huts?
A: Yes, some routes in the Espot area, particularly those exploring the higher alpine regions, may pass by mountain refuges. For instance, the 'Kilómetro Vertical' challenges in Vall de Peguera lead to the Josep Maria Blanc refuge. You can also find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/549567" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomèrs Refuge&lt;/a&gt; in the nearby lakes area.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Espot?
A: The best time for running in Espot is generally from late spring through early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is more favorable, and the high-altitude trails are typically free of snow, offering optimal conditions to enjoy the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of waterfalls?
A: While specific waterfalls are not highlighted in the immediate route data, the region's mountainous terrain and numerous lakes suggest the presence of natural water features. The lush forests and diverse landscapes of the Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici National Park often include streams and cascades, especially during spring melt.

Q: Can I find routes that combine running with exploring charming villages?
A: Yes, the region surrounding Espot, including the peripheral area of the National Park, features charming villages. While the core running routes focus on natural landscapes, you can plan your runs to start or end in these villages, allowing you to combine your physical activity with cultural exploration of their historical architecture.

Q: Are there any specific routes known for their unique geological features or rock formations?
A: The Pyrenees are known for their dramatic geological formations. While specific rock formations are not detailed for individual running routes, the challenging alpine terrain of routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20530468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pont de Pedra d'Espot – Río Escrita Stone Bridge loop from Espot&lt;/a&gt; will expose you to the rugged and varied geology of the high mountains, offering a visually stimulating experience.
tips: Located in the heart of the Escrita river valley, Espot is a town with typical Pyrenean architecture. Its artistic heritage includes buildings such as the parish church of Santa Llogaia and the Moros tower, which functioned as a defensive tower during the Middle Ages. The town constitutes the main entrance to the National Park of Aigüestortes and Estany de Sant Maurici, a rich natural space with rivers of turbulent waters, high mountain springs and more than a hundred "ponds", or lakes.
This beautiful river is a tributary of the Noguera Pallaresa.
The path is perfect for running and the views are great too.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the La Mola Trail?
faqs.answer: The La Mola Trail offers a varied terrain, including forested areas and paths with diverse vegetation. While the specific tour is rated as easy, you can expect a mix of surfaces suitable for jogging within the Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along this route, you'll encounter several points of interest. These include the Espot Sign, the picturesque Río Escrita Stone Bridge, and a rewarding View of the Pyrenees Mountains. The trail also offers diverse flora and fauna typical of the Pyrenean landscape.

faqs.question: Is the La Mola Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, this specific La Mola Trail is rated as easy, making it suitable for beginners and casual joggers. It covers a distance of approximately 4.3 kilometers with a modest elevation gain, designed to be accessible.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: On average, jogging the La Mola Trail takes approximately 28 to 29 minutes to complete the full loop, depending on your pace.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the La Mola Trail?
faqs.answer: The best time to visit the La Mola Trail, located within the Pyrenees, is generally during the warmer months from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these periods, the weather is more stable, and the trails are typically free of snow, offering the most pleasant jogging conditions.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route also passes through sections of the Ramat de Camins. Etapa 8 : Escaló => Llac de SantMaurici => Espot, Ramat de Camins. Etapa 9 : Espot => Esterri d'Àneu, and the Senda Pirenaica - E18.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for the Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici?
faqs.answer: While there isn't typically an entrance fee to access the Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici itself, certain activities or areas within the park might require specific permits or have regulations. It's always best to check the official park website or visitor centers for the most current information regarding any necessary permits, especially for activities like camping or specific guided tours.

faqs.question: Is the La Mola Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Regulations regarding dogs in national parks can vary. In the Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici, dogs are generally permitted on trails but must be kept on a leash at all times to protect wildlife and ensure the safety of other visitors. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Where does the La Mola Trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The La Mola Trail is a loop that starts and ends near Espot, within the Parque Nacional de Aigüestortes i Estany de Sant Maurici. Specific parking information for the trailhead should be confirmed with local visitor centers or park authorities, as access points and parking availability can change, especially during peak seasons.

faqs.question: What should I bring for jogging on this trail?
faqs.answer: For jogging on the La Mola Trail, we recommend bringing appropriate athletic footwear, comfortable clothing suitable for the weather, water to stay hydrated, and a small backpack for essentials. Given the varied terrain and potential for panoramic views, a camera might also be a good idea.

faqs.question: Are there any amenities near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail is located near Espot, a village that serves as a gateway to the national park. You can typically find basic amenities such as accommodation, restaurants, and shops in Espot, which can be convenient before or after your jog.
